Who was akbar?

Akbar, also known as Akbar the Great, was the third Mughal emperor of India, ruling from 1556 to 1605. He expanded the empire across much of the Indian subcontinent, promoted religious tolerance, and is renowned for his administrative reforms and patronage of art and culture. He is often regarded as one of history's greatest rulers.
